method_summary: The paper develops two attack methods for preference poisoning on offline RLHF: Binary-Aware Lattice Attack (BAL-A) using lattice reduction algorithms, and Binary Matching Pursuit Attack (BMP-A) adapting binary matching pursuit to non-normalized gradient dictionaries.
key_innovation: Conversion of the poisoning problem into a structured binary sparse approximation problem, enabling efficient attacks with binary coefficient constraints.
title: Efficient Preference Poisoning Attack on Offline RLHF
